what_it_does: Defines a static ConceptV icon library browser that loads icon SVG bodies, groups named icons into categories, lets users search and filter by category, adjust preview tone/style/size, and click an icon tile to copy its name.
observations: Declares a dsCard metadata comment identifying the page as a Brand icon explorer with a 1280x800 viewport.
Uses colors_and_type.css design tokens for typography, color, radii, shadows, easing, and surface styling.
Defines category-to-icon-name mappings directly in the page script.
Loads cv-icons.js and expects window.CV_ICONS.data to provide SVG body markup keyed by icon name.
Renders icon previews with three tones, three styles, and a size range from 20px to 64px.
Updates the visible icon count from the loaded icon data, while displayed groups come from the hard-coded category map.
Uses innerHTML to rebuild category sections and icon tiles on each render.
Copies the icon name through navigator.clipboard and shows a transient toast on success.
standouts: Single-page searchable icon catalog with live visual preview controls.
Explicit category taxonomy for the icon names used by the browser.
Copy-to-clipboard workflow centered on icon identifiers rather than SVG source.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from the design system instead of hard-coded theme values for most styling.
Uses inline SVG strings for icon rendering and a simple client-side state object for UI state.
Uses data attributes on controls and tiles to carry tone, style, category, and icon-name values.
Uses direct DOM event assignment and full content re-rendering rather than a framework.
Breaks strict separation of structure, style, and behavior by keeping page CSS and JavaScript inline.
concerns: The empty-state message injects the raw search query into innerHTML, so special characters are not escaped.
renderIcon assumes every displayed icon name exists in window.CV_ICONS.data.
Clipboard failure has no visible fallback or error state.
The count reflects all loaded icons, while the rendered catalog only covers names present in the category map.
notes: A self-contained handoff page for browsing, previewing, and copying ConceptV icon names.
questions: Should uncategorized icons from window.CV_ICONS.data appear somewhere in the browser?
Should failed clipboard writes show a fallback message?
Are category mappings intended to be manually maintained in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a searchable icon browser with category filters, preview controls, and click-to-copy icon names.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make the ConceptV icon set inspectable and easy to reference during implementation by exposing names, categories, preview variants, and copy behavior in one browser page.
novelty: Combines a static design-system asset catalog with live tone, wrapper style, and size preview controls plus name-copy behavior.
contribution: Provides an interactive, categorized icon inventory with search, visual preview controls, copyable icon identifiers, and a concrete mapping between category labels and icon names for a fused system to retain.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a static HTML/CSS/JavaScript ConceptV icon library explorer that renders categorized SVG icon tiles from window.CV_ICONS.data with search, filters, preview styling controls, and copy-to-clipboard behavior.
intention_for_embedding: It was for design-system handoff and implementation reference, letting users browse ConceptV icons visually and copy the exact icon name needed in code.
